description_long: This object is a vintage-style gas pump, predominantly painted in a weathered red with black patches and visible signs of rust and wear, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The pump features a prominent circular sign at the top with a red star logo on a white background, evoking classic Texaco branding. On one side, a metallic fuel nozzle and hose are mounted, with a small rectangular display panel and a metallic handle visible beneath. The body is rectangular with rounded edges, and the base is slightly wider for stability. The surface is mostly metal, with glass or plastic likely used for the display and logo sign. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of mid-20th-century American gas stations.
description: A weathered, vintage red gas pump with a circular star logo sign, metallic nozzle, and visible signs of rust and wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the pump's rectangular body, the circular star logo sign on top, and the fuel nozzle and hose mounted on the left.
description_view_1: Narrow side view showing mostly the black rear panel and the thickness of the pump, with the circular sign visible from the side.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the full face of the pump, the circular star logo sign, and the main display area with the nozzle and hose on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the fuel hose and nozzle in more detail, as well as the weathered red surface and some control elements.
attribute_fuel_pumps: One visible vintage-style fuel pump, painted red with black weathering, featuring a single metallic hose and nozzle mounted on the side, a small display panel, and a prominent circular star logo sign on top.
attribute_station_signage: A large circular sign with a red star logo is mounted on top of the pump, serving as the main branding element. No other signage is visible.
